name: multidict (Multi-value Dictionary for HTTP)

performance.notes: C extension in 6.x; very fast for HTTP header operations

agent_readiness.notes: Case-insensitive multi-value dictionary for HTTP headers and query strings. from multidict import MultiDict, CIMultiDict, MultiDictProxy. d = MultiDict([('key', 'value1'), ('key', 'value2')]). d['key'] → 'value1' (first). d.getall('key') → ['value1', 'value2']. d.get('missing', 'default'). CIMultiDict: case-insensitive — CIMultiDict({'Content-Type': 'text/html'}); d['content-type'] == d['Content-Type']. MultiDictProxy: immutable view. list(d.items()) → [('key', 'value1'), ('key', 'value2')]. Used internally by aiohttp for headers. d.popone('key') → first. CIMultiDictProxy for immutable case-insensitive. Type annotations. C extension for performance.

gotchas: d['key'] returns first value only — use getall() for all values
Primarily useful for HTTP headers/query strings; niche elsewhere
C extension may complicate some build environments
CIMultiDict vs MultiDict confusion when case sensitivity matters
best_when: Handling HTTP headers that appear multiple times (e.g., Set-Cookie)
Parsing URL query strings with repeated parameters
Building HTTP client/server libraries (used by aiohttp)
avoid_when: General-purpose data storage (use dict or defaultdict)
When single-value lookups are all that's needed
summary: multidict provides multi-value dict for HTTP headers. MultiDict([('k','v1'),('k','v2')]); d.getall('k') → ['v1','v2']. CIMultiDict for case-insensitive headers. MultiDictProxy for immutable. C extension in v6. Apache-2.0 by aio-libs. Used by aiohttp internals. Best for HTTP headers and repeated query params. d['key'] returns first value only — use getall() for all. Niche outside HTTP context.
